title: The AI race moved up to the software layer above the model
summary: Compute is the moat below and the agent software is the moat above, while the model in the middle commoditizes, so build the software layer and keep the model swappable.
takeaways: Build and own the software layer around the agent — its context, tools, and controls — and treat the model as a swappable input behind a routing seam.

The model is the part that commoditizes: open models now win token volume at about 1% of spend and the adoption leader flips month to month, so a stack wired to one model is anchored to the fastest-moving piece.
Route every model call through one gateway you control, keep context and controls model-agnostic, and a swap for a price cut or an outage becomes a config change instead of a rewrite.

sources.title: Vercel: AI Gateway Production Index, June 2026
SiliconANGLE: OpenAI acquires agent-orchestration startup Ona
TechCrunch: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock
Cohere: North Mini Code (first agentic coding model)
IBM: CIOs/CTOs face growing AI control gap
Kore.ai: enterprise AI agent risk survey

body:
Vercel publishes the model mix running through its AI gateway, and in one month the open model DeepSeek went [from under 1% to 17% of all tokens while holding around 1% of spend](https://vercel.com/blog/ai-gateway-production-index-june-2026). Volume and spend have decoupled, and the cheapest capable model keeps changing under production apps every few weeks. Wire your code straight to one provider's API and you are anchored to the part that moves the most.

That is because the AI race is splitting away from the model in three places at once. Underneath, compute is the moat: SpaceX absorbed xAI and priced the [largest IPO in history](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/11/spacex-officially-prices-shares-at-135-in-the-largest-ipo-ever/) on the strength of its supercomputers, then rented bridge GPU capacity to Google at [920 million dollars a month](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/05/google-will-pay-spacex-920m-per-month-for-compute/), so even a hyperscaler now leases compute. On top, the value is the software: OpenAI [bought the agent-runtime startup Ona](https://siliconangle.com/2026/06/11/openai-acquires-ai-agent-orchestration-startup-ona/) to run long agent tasks, and SpaceX [bought the coding tool Cursor for sixty billion dollars in stock](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/16/spacex-to-acquire-cursor-for-60b-in-stock-days-after-blockbuster-ipo/). Even model labs moved into the software, with Cohere shipping [its first agentic coding model](https://cohere.com/blog/north-mini-code) and new startups pivoting straight into the layer: agent identity at [NewCore](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/15/ai-agents-are-becoming-employees-newcore-emerges-with-66m-to-give-them-identities/), agent context at [Jedify](https://techcrunch.com/2026/06/10/jedify-raises-24m-to-help-companies-arm-ai-agents-with-context-on-their-business/).

In the middle, the model commoditizes. Anthropic just edged past OpenAI in US business adoption, [41% to 39.5%](https://ramp.com/leading-indicators/ai-index-june-2026); even OpenAI's own leaked financials show [revenue growing to about 13 billion dollars while losses ran larger](https://arstechnica.com/ai/2026/06/leaked-financial-docs-show-openai-is-losing-billions-of-dollars-a-year/), and venture money is piling into the same two names, with [about 88% of 2026 AI funding going to US companies](https://news.crunchbase.com/venture/us-ai-startup-funding-boom-data/). What does not commoditize is the software around the agent, and it is where teams are most exposed: IBM found [77% of leaders say adoption already outpaces their governance](https://newsroom.ibm.com/2026-06-08-new-ibm-study-finds-cios-and-ctos-face-growing-ai-control-gap-as-enterprise-deployment-scales), Bain [only 7% run fully autonomous agents in production](https://www.bain.com/insights/your-ai-budget-is-growing-your-returns-arent-heres-why/), and in one survey [79% had already reversed an agent's action](https://www.kore.ai/news/new-kore-ai-survey-72-of-enterprises-say-their-ai-agents-operate-with-unmanaged-risk-and-create-new-operational-burdens).

The concrete move: put a routing seam between your app and the providers, a gateway every model call passes through, and keep the context, tools, and controls in your own layer behind it. That seam is also where your spend caps, audit log, and model fallback live. Then swapping a model, for a price cut or an outage, is a config change, and the layer you built stays yours.

[Architecture & Orchestration](/guide/architecture-and-orchestration/)
